evals.prompt: My reports keep erroring out. Is something wrong with my setup?
evals.expected_output: Should route to report id setup-check, which checks Google login, scopes, local configuration, and saved defaults before running reports. Should follow the discovery flow: seo_list_reports with category setup, seo_describe_report with id setup-check, then seo_run_report. Should note the report takes no parameters. Should read ok first, then each checks entry status, distinguishing fail from warn, with its detail and fix, then generatedAt.
assertions: Uses report id setup-check to diagnose the local setup
Discovers or identifies setup-check through the report catalog (MCP tools or seo reports list/describe/run)
Notes the report takes no parameters
Reads ok first, then each check status
Distinguishes fail from warn and reads each detail and fix
Presents the diagnosis from the actual check results rather than generic setup advice

evals.prompt: Agent task: run the local setup check and tell me exactly what to fix.
evals.expected_output: Should run report id setup-check with no parameters, since the schema is empty, via seo reports run setup-check or seo_run_report. Should read each checks entry, separate fail from warn, and surface the detail and fix for each failed check. Should recommend applying the fix, re-running setup-check, then running a small read-only report for the intended property.
assertions: Runs report id setup-check with no parameters
Separates fail from warn across the checks
Surfaces the detail and fix for each failed check
Recommends applying the fix and re-running setup-check
Recommends a small read-only report after setup passes

evals.prompt: Setup check passed, so my token works, I can pull data for any property, and the site's SEO is fine.
evals.expected_output: Should refuse the claims. A passing result means local prerequisites look present, not that a token is still accepted by Google or that the account can access a property. A setup pass is not evidence about rankings, indexing, or site health. Should recommend running a small read-only report for the intended property to confirm access.
assertions: Refuses to read a pass as a working token or property access
States a pass means local prerequisites look present, not accepted by Google
States a setup pass is not evidence about rankings, indexing, or site health
Recommends a small read-only report to confirm property access

evals.prompt: It warns about missing saved defaults, so that is a blocking failure, right?
evals.expected_output: Should not treat the warning as a blocking failure. Missing saved defaults are usually warnings because explicit parameters can replace them. Should distinguish warn from fail and note a report can still run when the site is passed explicitly rather than from a saved default.
assertions: Refuses to treat the missing-defaults warning as a blocking failure
States missing saved defaults are usually warnings because explicit parameters can replace them
Notes a report can run with the site passed explicitly
